reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All gates passed. Code verification: VerifyDataColumnSidecar (p2p_interface.go:34-52) checks len(kzg_commitments) > 0 and array length consistency, but does NOT validate len(kzg_commitments) <= max_blobs_per_block. The max_blobs_per_block check exists in DataColumnsAlignWithBlock (verification.go:28-38) but is only called during reconstruction (reconstruction.go:229), not during gossip validation (validate_data_column.go:81). 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json PROP-6a4369e9-inv-009 requires 'kzg_commitments count must not exceed max_blobs_per_block for the sidecar's epoch.' Severity: An attacker can gossip oversized data column sidecars (e.g., 100 commitments vs. max 6/9/12) via P2P, causing nodes to allocate excessive memory and CPU for KZG batch verification (verification.go:61-92 allocates 4 arrays sized by total commitment count across sidecars). With Prysm at 31% network share, sustained exploitation could bring down >33% of the network, meeting High severity threshold (BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json:189-197).
